title: Daily Briefing — Sunday, May 24
tldr: DeepSeek made its 75% V4-Pro price cut permanent — frontier-class reasoning at ~¼ the cost of comparable US tokens.
Claude Code escaped the terminal — now usable from any browser and on iPhone.
“Claude Mythos” launch code surfaced on GitHub, hinting at a sooner-than-expected release of Anthropic's advanced security model.

blocks.p: DeepSeek confirmed the temporary 75% discount on V4-Pro is now permanent — roughly a quarter the cost of equivalent OpenAI/Anthropic tokens.
blocks.doIt: Re-run your build-vs-buy and inference-cost math; an ultra-cheap open-weight option changes the calculus for high-volume tasks.

blocks.p: Anthropic shipped Claude Code to the browser and iPhone for the first time, moving agentic coding beyond the terminal.
blocks.why: Shrinking the surface area expands who can run agents — and from where.
blocks.doIt: Kick off a task from your phone and review the diff later; treat agents as async coworkers.

blocks.p: Launch code for Claude Mythos surfaced on GitHub, suggesting an earlier release; reporting also noted US intelligence agencies keep using Claude even after Anthropic was flagged as a national-security concern over Mythos's cyber capabilities.
blocks.why: The most capable security models are becoming geopolitically sensitive, dual-use assets.

sources: buildfastwithai (May 24 roundup) · DeepSeek · Anthropic · GitHub (figures directional where noted).
